tool_calls:
cat << 'EOF' > /project/build.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

DEPS_FILE="/project/deps.txt"
OUT_DIR="/project/out"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

# ── 1. Parse deps.txt into associative arrays ──────────────────────────────
declare -A deps   # deps[target]="dep1 dep2 ..."
declare -A in_degree
declare -a all_targets

while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
  target="${line%%:*}"
  target="${target// /}"
  rest="${line#*:}"
  deps["$target"]="${rest# }"   # may be empty string
  all_targets+=("$target")
done < "$DEPS_FILE"

# Initialise in-degree counters
for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  in_degree["$t"]=0
done

# Count how many declared targets depend on each node
for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do
    (( in_degree["$t"]++ )) || true   # actually count t's own deps
    :
  done
done

# Re-compute correctly: in_degree[t] = number of deps t still needs
declare -A remaining
for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  count=0
  for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do
    (( count++ )) || true
  done
  remaining["$t"]=$count
done

# ── 2. Topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) ─────────────────────────────────
declare -a order=()
declare -a queue=()

# Seed queue with nodes that have no dependencies
for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t")
done

while [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
  # Pop front
  node="${queue[0]}"
  queue=("${queue[@]:1}")
  order+=("$node")

  # For every other target that lists `node` as a dep, decrement its counter
  for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
    for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do
      if [[ "$dep" == "$node" ]]; then
        (( remaining["$t"]-- )) || true
        [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t")
      fi
    done
  done
done

# Cycle / missing-dep check
if [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected – cannot determine build order." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ── 3. Build each target in order ──────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Build order: ${order[*]}"
echo "──────────────────────────────"
for target in "${order[@]}"; do
  echo "  Building $target …"
  echo "built: $target" > "$OUT_DIR/$target"
  echo "  ✔  $OUT_DIR/$target written"
done
echo "──────────────────────────────"
echo "All targets built successfully."
EOF
chmod +x /project/build.sh

bash /project/build.sh

echo ""
echo "── Verify output files ───────────────────────"
for f in /project/out/*; do
  echo "$f → $(cat "$f")"
done

# Debug: check how the file was parsed
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  echo "LINE: [$line]"
  target="${line%%:*}"
  target="${target// /}"
  echo "  target=[$target]"
done < /project/deps.txt

# The issue: all_targets is populated inside the while loop's subshell (pipe).
# Fix: use process substitution instead of a pipe so variables persist in the current shell.
cat << 'EOF' > /project/build.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

DEPS_FILE="/project/deps.txt"
OUT_DIR="/project/out"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

# ── 1. Parse deps.txt ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
declare -A deps        # deps[target]="dep1 dep2 ..."
declare -a all_targets

# Use process substitution (< <(...)) so the while loop runs in *this* shell,
# keeping array assignments visible after the loop.
while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
  target="${line%%:*}"
  target="${target//[[:space:]]/}"
  rest="${line#*:}"
  rest="${rest# }"   # strip leading space; may be empty
  deps["$target"]="$rest"
  all_targets+=("$target")
done < <(grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' "$DEPS_FILE")

# ── 2. Topological sort – Kahn's algorithm ─────────────────────────────────
declare -A remaining   # remaining[t] = number of unbuilt deps

for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  count=0
  for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do
    (( count++ )) || true
  done
  remaining["$t"]=$count
done

declare -a order=()
declare -a queue=()

for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
  [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t")
done

while [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
  node="${queue[0]}"
  queue=("${queue[@]:1}")
  order+=("$node")

  for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do
    for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do
      if [[ "$dep" == "$node" ]]; then
        (( remaining["$t"]-- )) || true
        [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t")
      fi
    done
  done
done

if [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ── 3. Build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "Build order: ${order[*]}"
echo "──────────────────────────────"
for target in "${order[@]}"; do
  echo "  Building $target …"
  echo "built: $target" > "$OUT_DIR/$target"
  echo "  ✔  $OUT_DIR/$target"
done
echo "──────────────────────────────"
echo "All targets built successfully."
EOF
chmod +x /project/build.sh

rm -f /project/out/*
bash /project/build.sh

echo ""
echo "── Verify output files ───────────────────────"
for f in /project/out/*; do
  echo "  $f  →  $(cat "$f")"
done

# Deep debug: check associative array contents after the loop
bash -c '
declare -A deps
declare -a all_targets

while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
  target="${line%%:*}"
  target="${target//[[:space:]]/}"
  rest="${line#*:}"
  rest="${rest# }"
  deps["$target"]="$rest"
  all_targets+=("$target")
done < <(grep -v "^[[:space:]]*$" /project/deps.txt)

echo "all_targets count: ${#all_targets[@]}"
echo "all_targets: ${all_targets[*]}"
for k in "${!deps[@]}"; do echo "  deps[$k]=[${deps[$k]}]"; done
'

# Check bash version – older bash may lack certain features
bash --version

# Test what actually works in this environment
declare -A mymap
mymap["foo"]="bar"
echo "map: ${mymap[foo]}"

declare -a myarr
myarr+=("a")
myarr+=("b")
echo "arr: ${myarr[*]}"
echo "len: ${#myarr[@]}"

# Test reading file with process substitution
declare -a lines
while IFS= read -r line; do
  lines+=("$line")
done < <(cat /project/deps.txt)
echo "lines: ${#lines[@]}"
for l in "${lines[@]}"; do echo "  [$l]"; done

tool_outputs.stdout:
app: lib utils
lib: core
utils: core
core:

tool_outputs.commands:
